name: cryptcat
title: cryptcat
summary: lightweight version netcat extended with twofish encryption
description:
lightweight version netcat extended with twofish encryption
Cryptcat is a simple Unix utility which reads and writes data across
network connections, using TCP or UDP protocol while encrypting the
data being transmitted.
It is designed to be a reliable "back-end" tool that can be used directly
or easily driven by other programs and scripts.  At the same time, it is a
feature-rich network debugging and exploration tool, since it can create
almost any kind of connection you would need and has several interesting
built-in capabilities.

commands.name: cryptcat
commands.description: twofish encryption enabled version of nc(1)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cryptcat because lightweight version netcat extended with twofish encryption.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cryptcat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cryptcat -k secret=$KFIOSA_SECRET hostname port[s] [ports]` as documented upstream for cryptcat.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cryptcat -k secret=$KFIOSA_SECRET -p port [-options] [hostname] [port]` as documented upstream for cryptcat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cryptcat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cryptcat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

Further invocations from upstream: `cryptcat -k secret=$KFIOSA_SECRET -p port [-options] [hostname] [port]`. lightweight version netcat extended with twofish encryption.
